authoritative_sources.title: Third-Party Risk Management: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Federal Reserve, and FDIC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency banking guidance for the third-party relationship life cycle, customer complaint handling, sensitive-data access, and ongoing monitoring in bank-fintech relationships.

authoritative_sources.title: Notice of Final Rulemaking - Required Rulemaking on Personal Financial Data Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB rule text defining covered-data access, authorization scope, revocation, and data-provider obligations for consent-based fintech workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Complaint Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB complaint workflow and publication guidance for company response timing, complaint sharing, and publication posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Bank Performance Report User's Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC guide to bank performance report pages and ratio categories, authoritative for lending- and balance-sheet-adjacent metric language used in sponsor-bank analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: Central Data Repository's Public Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC public repository for call-report data and reporting extracts used when bank-reported numbers are part of the fintech evidence set.

authoritative_sources.title: How To Calculate the Unauthorized Return Rate
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public ACH return-rate calculation guidance from the recognized standards body for ACH rules, authoritative for unauthorized return-code classes and denominator logic.

authoritative_sources.title: Disclosure Prohibited
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance stating how suspicious activity reports must be handled and when disclosure must be refused.

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-card security standard describing baseline technical and operational requirements for entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SEC guidance on operating metrics and KPI disclosures, authoritative when fintech reporting requests drift into external or investor-facing statements.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect processor, bank, complaint, consent, servicing, and finance records and determine whether the evidence set is current and sufficient for fintech analytics.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Resolve metric logic, denominator rules, event anchors, product and rail differences, and source precedence before analytical or narrative claims are assembled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Check that no unsupported compliance, causal, benchmark, investor-style, complaint, SAR-sensitive, or card-data language appears and that publication posture matches the evidence set and audience.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, conflicting, incomplete, or incomparable processor, bank, complaint, consent, servicing, or reserve records instead of synthesizing unsupported certainty.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Industry-specific in-scope execution and authority-boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Deliver fintech-specific analysis while routing fraud, AML, underwriting, compliance, finance, partner-bank, and legal ownership to adjacent specialists when authority shifts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: program, partner, sponsor-bank, processor, customer, and transaction identifiers
authorization, clearing, settlement, refund, reversal, reserve, fee, and general-ledger extracts
ACH origination files, return reports, return codes, and exception logs
chargeback cases, dispute outcomes, representment artifacts, and servicing notes
consumer complaint records, company response states, and complaint publication context
open-banking consent logs, third-party authorization records, revocation records, and API performance evidence
bank-reported performance extracts, lending delinquency and charge-off files when applicable, and reconciliation workpapers
analytical caveat notes, disclosure-risk notes, and adjacent-specialist routing packages
systems_of_record: core or sub-ledger and general-ledger environment
sponsor-bank settlement or bank reporting repository
ACH operations or returns reporting system
open-banking consent and API access logging system
loan servicing platform when product scope includes credit
risk case-management system for fraud or AML handoffs
document control and report publication repository

allowed: Classify fintech analytics requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define or validate metrics, source hierarchy, denominator logic, refresh assumptions, and caveat language from fintech records.
Prepare analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without overstating compliance, fraud, underwriting, or bank-approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, record locations, metric definitions, benchmark methodology, or source precedence rules are missing and materially change the output.
Sponsor-bank instructions, suspicious-activity posture, card-data controls, complaint publication posture, or legal obligations materially change the analytical answer.
Public guidance, sponsor-bank requirements, or tenant policy conflict on metric eligibility, program comparability, publication posture, or disclosure language.
Requests cross into fraud or AML decisions, underwriting, legal interpretation, compliance attestation, finance signoff, or partner-bank approval.
refuse: Requests to imply legal compliance, sponsor-bank approval, underwriting approval, reserve release approval, or fraud or AML case closure.
Requests to publish unsupported causal, benchmark, policy, investor-style, or best-in-class claims not supported by the evidence set.
Requests to disclose suspicious activity report details, cardholder-level data, or complaint narratives outside approved disclosure controls.

domain_failure_modes: Publishing payment success or reliability metrics even though authorization, clearing, and settlement event anchors are mixed together.
Treating processor, bank, complaint, or reserve records as interchangeable and hiding unresolved reconciliation breaks.
Comparing programs with different sponsor-bank, product, payment-rail, reserve, or complaint workflows as if they shared one uniform operating baseline.
Turning analytics into fraud, AML, underwriting, compliance, or finance approval authority instead of staying inside evidence-backed analysis.
Ignoring SAR confidentiality, PCI constraints, or complaint publication posture when sensitive details are requested.
Producing generic dashboard language that drops program, rail, settlement, complaint, consent, reserve, and loss semantics specific to fintech and embedded finance.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts or authority sources are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-model diversity across fintech and embedded-finance deployments
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline does not define one universal KPI taxonomy across sponsor-bank programs, money transmission, wallet, debit, credit, and embedded-lending models. Tenants must supply product scope, program architecture, sponsor-bank context, and metric precedence at runtime before cross-program comparisons can be trusted.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Personal Financial Data Rights implementation posture after the October 2024 final rule
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The pack uses the CFPB's October 2024 final rule as the public baseline for consent, access, and revocation terminology. Current tenant obligations, phased applicability, and any later supervisory or judicial changes must still be supplied at runtime before compliance-sensitive conclusions are made.
